english: I'm trying to get to the subway station. Am I going the right way?
alt: Is this the right direction for the subway?
note: Am I going the right way? = 이 방향 맞아?

english: How long does it take to get to the airport?
alt: How far is the airport from here?
note: How long = 시간 / How far = 거리

english: You can drop me off right here. Thanks.
alt: This is my stop. Thanks for the ride.
note: Thanks for the ride = 태워줘서 고마워

korean: 와이파이 비밀번호 좀 알 수 있을까요?
english: What's the Wi-Fi password by any chance?
alt: Could I get the Wi-Fi info?

english: Hi I have a reservation under Park. I'd like to check in.
alt: I'm checking in. The reservation is under Park.

english: I missed my flight. Is there a later one I can get on?
alt: I didn't make my flight. What are my options?
note: What are my options?은 대안을 물을 때 유용

korean: 저희 사진 좀 찍어주실 수 있으세요?
english: Would you mind taking a picture of us?
alt: Could you snap a quick photo of us?
note: Would you mind~?가 가장 정중한 표현

english: How long does it usually take to get downtown?
alt: What's the average commute time to downtown?

korean: 친구랑 같이 앉고 싶은데 자리 바꿔주실 수 있나요?
english: Would you mind switching seats so I can sit with my friend?
alt: Could we swap seats? I'd love to sit next to my friend.

korean: 식당까지 걸어갈 만해 아니면 차 가져갈까?
english: Is the restaurant within walking distance or should we drive?
alt: Can we walk there or is it too far?
note: within walking distance = 걸어갈 수 있는 거리

english: Do I need to transfer or is it a direct line?
alt: Is it a straight shot or do I have to switch trains?
note: straight shot = 직통 / switch lines = 환승하다

korean: 다음 정거장에서 내려서 3번 출구로 나가면 돼
english: Get off at the next stop and take exit 3.
alt: Your stop is next. Head out through exit 3.
note: the stop after next = 다다음 정거장

english: Is there a pharmacy near here?
alt: Do you know if there's a drugstore around here?

english: Where can I park around here?
alt: Is there parking nearby?
note: Is there parking? = 주차할 데 있어?

english: Does this bus go to city hall?
alt: Is this the right bus for city hall?
note: Am I on the right bus? = 이 버스 맞아?

english: Where can I buy a ticket?
alt: Where do I get tickets?

english: What time is checkout?
alt: When do we have to be out by?

korean: 거기 가려면 차 없으면 좀 힘들어
english: It's kind of hard to get there without a car.
alt: That place is pretty inaccessible if you don't drive.

korean: 살짝 멀미 날 것 같아 창문 좀 열어줄래?
english: I'm feeling a little carsick. Can you crack the window?
alt: I think I might get motion sickness. Can we open a window?
note: carsick = 차멀미 / crack the window = 창문 살짝 열다

korean: 이 동네 치안이 별로라 밤에 혼자 다니지 마
english: This neighborhood isn't that safe. Don't walk alone at night.
alt: The area's a bit sketchy. Stick to well-lit streets after dark.

korean: 거기 주차 자리 있어? 주차하기 힘들면 대중교통 타자
english: Is there parking there? If it's hard to park let's just take public transit.
alt: Any parking available? If not let's take the subway instead.

english: The flight's been delayed. We have to wait two more hours.
alt: Our plane is running two hours late. What a pain.

korean: 혹시 근처에 약국 어디 있는지 아세요?
english: Do you happen to know if there's a pharmacy nearby?
alt: Excuse me is there a drugstore around here?

korean: 우리 여기서 사진 찍자 배경 진짜 예쁘다
english: Let's take a photo here. The background is gorgeous.
alt: We have to get a picture here. This view is stunning.

korean: 택시비가 이렇게 비싸? 그냥 지하철 탈걸
english: The taxi fare is that much? I should've just taken the subway.
alt: That's highway robbery for a cab ride. Should've gone with the metro.

korean: 거기 가본 적 있어? 후기 좀 알려줘
english: Have you been there before? Give me your honest review.
alt: You've been right? How was it? Worth going?

english: What time is check-in? We arrived a little early.
alt: We're here ahead of schedule. When can we check in?

korean: 거기 에어비앤비로 잡았어 호텔보다 훨씬 싸더라
english: I booked an Airbnb there. Way cheaper than a hotel.
alt: We're staying at an Airbnb. It was a fraction of the hotel price.

korean: 이번 여행에서 가장 좋았던 게 뭐야?
english: What was the best part of the trip for you?
alt: What was the highlight of your vacation?

korean: 와 여기 야경 미쳤다 사진으로는 안 담긴다
english: The night view here is breathtaking. Photos don't do it justice.
alt: This view is insane at night. You have to see it in person.
note: don't do it justice = 실물만큼 안 나오다

korean: 여기 팁 얼마나 줘야 해? 미국은 팁 문화가 달라서 헷갈려
english: How much should I tip here? Tipping culture in the US is so confusing.
alt: What's the standard tip? I always get confused with American tipping.

korean: 여기 무료 주차 되나요? 아니면 근처에 주차장 있어요?
english: Is parking free here? Or is there a parking lot nearby?
alt: Do I need to pay for parking or is it complimentary?

korean: 나 길치라서 네비 없으면 진짜 길 못 찾아
english: I have zero sense of direction. I'm completely lost without GPS.
alt: I'm so bad with directions. Without navigation I'd be wandering forever.
note: sense of direction = 방향 감각 / GPS = 네비게이션

english: Are pets allowed in here?
alt: Is this place pet-friendly?

korean: 여기서 걸어서 갈 수 있어? 아니면 택시 타야 해?
english: Can we walk there from here or should we grab a cab?
alt: Is it walking distance or do we need a ride?
note: walking distance = 걸어갈 수 있는 거리 / grab a cab = 택시 타다

korean: 이거 짐 맡아줄 수 있는 곳 있나요?
english: Is there somewhere I can store my luggage?
alt: Do you have a luggage storage service?

korean: 이거 환율 얼마야? 원화로 얼마 정도 되는 거야?
english: What's the exchange rate? How much is this in Korean won?
alt: How does this convert to won at the current rate?

english: I'm terrified of flying. The anxiety is unbearable.
alt: I have such a bad fear of flying. I'm freaking out.
note: terrified = 무서운 / fear of flying = 비행 공포증

korean: 여기서 사진 찍으면 안 되나요? 촬영 금지예요?
english: Are photos not allowed here? Is there a no photography policy?
alt: Can I take pictures here or is it restricted?
note: no photography = 촬영 금지 / restricted = 제한된

korean: 주말에 캠핑 가려고 하는데 날씨가 좀 걸리네
english: I'm thinking of going camping this weekend but the weather has me worried.
alt: We want to camp this weekend but the forecast doesn't look great.
note: forecast = 일기예보 / has me worried = 걱정되게 하다

korean: 출발 전에 화장실 다녀와 중간에 쉴 데 없어
english: Use the bathroom before we leave. There won't be any stops along the way.
alt: Hit the restroom now. We're not stopping until we get there.

korean: 너 운전 좀 천천히 해 무서워
english: Slow down a bit. You're scaring me.
alt: Can you ease up on the gas? I'm getting nervous.
note: ease up = 줄이다/천천히 하다 / on the gas = 가속 페달에

korean: 나 지금 교통카드 잔액이 없어 충전 어디서 해?
english: My transit card is empty. Where can I top it up?
alt: I need to reload my bus pass. Is there a kiosk nearby?
note: top up = 충전하다 / reload = 재충전 / kiosk = 키오스크

korean: 거기 가려면 환승 한 번 해야 해
english: You need to transfer once to get there.
alt: Take line 2 and switch at the next station.

korean: 시차 적응이 안 돼서 낮에 졸리고 밤에 잠이 안 와
english: I'm still jet-lagged. Sleepy during the day and wide awake at night.
alt: The time difference is messing with me. My sleep schedule is all over the place.
note: jet-lagged = 시차 적응 안 된 / all over the place = 엉망인

korean: 여기 화장실 깨끗한 편이야? 별로면 좀 참을게
english: Are the restrooms here decent? If not I'll hold it.
alt: How clean are the bathrooms? I'd rather wait if they're gross.
note: decent = 괜찮은 / hold it = 참다 / gross = 더러운

korean: 그래서 우리 이번 휴가 어디로 갈까? 후보 있어?
english: So where should we go for our vacation? Any ideas?
alt: Have you thought about where you want to travel? I'm open to suggestions.

korean: 짐이 너무 무거워 택배로 보내는 게 낫겠다
english: This luggage is way too heavy. It'd be better to just ship it.
alt: My bags weigh a ton. Let's mail the heavy stuff instead.
note: ship/mail = 배송하다 / weigh a ton = 엄청 무겁다

korean: 여기 에스컬레이터는 왼쪽으로 서는 거야 오른쪽으로 서는 거야?
english: Do you stand on the left or right side of the escalator here?
alt: Which side do you stand on here? Every country is different.

korean: 이 근처에 ATM 어디 있어요? 현금이 필요해서
english: Is there an ATM around here? I need to get some cash.
alt: Where's the nearest cash machine? I need to withdraw some money.

korean: 거기 교통편이 어때? 대중교통으로 갈 수 있어?
english: How's the transportation there? Is it accessible by public transit?
alt: Can you get there by bus or subway or do you need a car?
note: accessible = 접근 가능한 / public transit = 대중교통

english: The airplane food was actually better than expected.
alt: I was pleasantly surprised by the in-flight meal.
note: in-flight meal = 기내식 / pleasantly surprised = 뜻밖에 좋아서 놀란

korean: 지금 현재 환율로 100달러면 한국 돈으로 얼마야?
english: How much is a hundred dollars in Korean won at the current rate?
alt: What's the won equivalent of a hundred bucks right now?
note: current rate = 현재 환율 / equivalent = 동등한 금액

korean: 주말 농장 체험 같이 갈래? 딸기 따기 한대
english: Want to go to a farm experience this weekend? They have strawberry picking.
alt: How about we go strawberry picking at that weekend farm?
note: strawberry picking = 딸기 따기 / farm experience = 농장 체험

korean: 이 길로 가면 맞아? 네비가 이상한 데로 안내하는 것 같은데
english: Is this the right way? The GPS seems to be taking us somewhere weird.
alt: I think the navigation is leading us astray. This doesn't look right.
note: leading astray = 잘못 안내하는 / GPS = 네비게이션

korean: 일본어는 좀 할 줄 알아? 거기 여행 갈 건데
english: Do you speak any Japanese? I'm planning a trip there.
alt: Know any Japanese? It might come in handy for our trip.

korean: 주차장이 꽉 찼어 다른 데 찾아봐야겠다
english: The parking lot is full. We need to find somewhere else.
alt: No spots left. Let's circle around and look for street parking.
note: circle around = 돌다 / street parking = 노상 주차

korean: 지하철 몇 호선 타야 해? 환승은 어디서 해?
english: Which subway line do I take? Where do I transfer?
alt: What line should I get on and where do I need to switch?
note: subway line = 지하철 호선 / transfer/switch = 환승

korean: 이 가방 기내 반입 되나요? 크기 제한이 어떻게 돼요?
english: Can I take this bag as a carry-on? What's the size limit?
alt: Does this meet the carry-on dimensions? I don't want to check it.

english: Is it possible to upgrade my seat? To business class?
alt: Can I get bumped up to business class? What would that cost?

english: We need to get off at the next stop. Get ready.
alt: Our stop is coming up. Let's move to the door.
note: get off = 내리다 / coming up = 다가오는

korean: 여기 진짜 한적하다 사람이 하나도 없네
english: This place is so peaceful. There's literally nobody here.
alt: It's completely deserted here. We have the whole place to ourselves.
note: deserted = 인적 없는 / have it to ourselves = 우리만 있는

korean: 이 동네 밤에 걸어도 안전해? 골목이 좀 어두운데
english: Is it safe to walk around here at night? The alleys are kind of dark.
alt: How safe is this neighborhood after dark? Some of these streets look sketchy.
note: after dark = 해가 진 후 / sketchy = 위험해 보이는

english: I missed the train. When's the next one?
alt: We just missed it. How long until the next train?

korean: 여기서 환전할 수 있나요? 달러를 원화로 바꾸고 싶은데
english: Can I exchange currency here? I'd like to change dollars to won.
alt: Where can I convert my dollars to Korean won?

korean: 좀 걸어 다니면서 구경하자 택시 타면 못 보잖아
english: Let's walk around and explore. You miss everything in a taxi.
alt: Let's go on foot and soak in the sights. You can't do that from a cab.
note: soak in = 느끼다/흡수하다 / on foot = 걸어서

korean: 나 멀미약 챙겼어 배 타면 항상 멀미해서
english: I brought motion sickness pills. I always get seasick on boats.
alt: I packed Dramamine just in case. Boats and I don't mix.

korean: 좌석 바꿔주실 수 있나요? 창가 자리로 부탁드려요
english: Could I switch to a window seat please?
alt: Is there any chance I can move to a window seat?
note: window seat = 창가 좌석 / switch = 바꾸다

korean: 이 지역 특산물 뭐가 유명해? 기념품으로 사가려고
english: What local specialties are famous here? I want to buy some as souvenirs.
alt: What's this area known for? I'd like to bring back something authentic.
note: local specialties = 지역 특산물 / souvenirs = 기념품

english: Is there a cancellation fee if I cancel this reservation?
alt: Do I get charged a penalty for cancelling?
note: cancellation fee = 취소 수수료 / penalty = 위약금

korean: 이거 어느 방향으로 가야 해? 출구가 어딘지 모르겠어
english: Which way should I go? I can't figure out where the exit is.
alt: I'm completely turned around. Where's the way out?
note: turned around = 방향감각 잃은 / way out = 출구

korean: 사람 많은 데서는 가방 앞으로 매 소매치기 조심해
english: Keep your bag in front of you in crowded areas. Watch out for pickpockets.
alt: Be careful with your belongings in crowds. Pickpockets are common here.

korean: 거기 주변에 볼거리 있어? 근처에서 시간 때울 데가 필요해
english: Is there anything to see around there? I need something to kill time nearby.
alt: Any attractions or spots worth visiting in the area?
note: kill time = 시간 때우다 / attractions = 볼거리

english: Is parking free here or do you have to pay?
alt: Do they charge for parking or is it complimentary?
note: complimentary = 무료 / charge for = 돈을 받다

korean: 여기서 사진 찍어도 되는 거야? 촬영 금지는 아니지?
english: Is it okay to take pictures here? It's not a no-photo zone right?
alt: Are we allowed to photograph here? I don't want to get in trouble.

korean: 거기 가면 꼭 먹어봐야 하는 게 뭐야?
english: What's a must-try when you go there?
alt: What should I absolutely not miss eating there?
note: must-try = 꼭 먹어봐야 하는 / not miss = 놓치지 않아야 할

korean: 거기 짐 보관할 곳 있어? 체크아웃 했는데 비행기가 밤이라
english: Is there luggage storage? I've already checked out but my flight isn't until tonight.
alt: Any place to keep our bags? We checked out but we still have hours to kill.
note: luggage storage = 짐 보관소 / hours to kill = 남는 시간

korean: 이번에 해외여행 가면 로밍할 거야 현지 유심 살 거야?
english: Are you going to use roaming on your overseas trip or buy a local SIM?
alt: Will you get an international plan or just grab a SIM card when you land?
note: roaming = 로밍 / local SIM = 현지 유심

korean: 좌석 예매 했어? 혹시 취소표 나올 수도 있으니까 계속 확인해봐
english: Did you book seats? Keep checking in case there are cancellations.
alt: I'd suggest refreshing the page for cancellation tickets. They pop up randomly.

korean: 택시 기본요금이 얼마야? 미터기 켜고 가는 거 맞지?
english: What's the base fare for a taxi? They use the meter right?
alt: How much is the starting rate? Make sure the meter is running.

korean: 거기 가면 기념 사진 꼭 찍어 나중에 후회해
english: Make sure you take photos there. You'll regret it if you don't.
alt: Don't forget to snap some pics. You'll want to remember that moment.
note: snap some pics = 사진 찍다 / regret = 후회하다

korean: 이거 가지고 비행기 탈 수 있어? 액체류 규정이 어떻게 돼?
english: Can I take this on a plane? What are the liquid restrictions?
alt: Does this fall within the carry-on liquid limits?
note: liquid restrictions = 액체류 규정 / carry-on = 기내

korean: 와 이 뷰 진짜 돈 값 한다
english: This view is worth every penny.
alt: The scenery alone justifies the price.
note: worth every penny = 돈 값을 하는 / justifies = 정당화하다

korean: 짐 다 쌌어? 여권이랑 충전기 챙겼어?
english: All packed? Did you remember your passport and charger?
alt: Double-check that you've got your passport and charger before we leave.
note: all packed = 다 싼 / double-check = 다시 확인

english: Be careful on these stairs. They're pretty steep.
alt: Watch out going down. The steps are steeper than they look.

korean: 이 구간 통행료가 얼마야? 현금으로 내야 해?
english: What's the toll for this section? Do I have to pay cash?
alt: How much is the road toll? Can I pay by card?

korean: 이 자리에서 일몰 보면 진짜 예쁘겠다
english: The sunset from this spot must be absolutely beautiful.
alt: Imagine watching the sunset from right here. It'd be breathtaking.
note: sunset = 일몰 / breathtaking = 숨 막히게 아름다운

korean: 짐 무게 초과하면 추가 요금 내야 해
english: You have to pay extra if your luggage is overweight.
alt: There's an excess baggage fee if you go over the weight limit.
note: overweight luggage = 초과 수하물 / excess baggage fee = 초과 수하물 요금

korean: 와 공기 진짜 좋다 역시 시골이 다르네
english: The air here is so fresh. There's nothing like the countryside.
alt: You can really feel the difference in air quality out here. City air has nothing on this.
note: countryside = 시골 / has nothing on = ~에 비할 바 아닌

korean: 거기 입장료 얼마야? 학생 할인 있어?
english: How much is the entrance fee? Is there a student discount?
alt: What's the admission price? Do they have reduced rates for students?
note: entrance fee / admission = 입장료 / student discount = 학생 할인

english: Can I use this card for international purchases?
alt: Does this card work for overseas transactions?
note: international purchases = 해외 결제 / overseas transactions = 해외 거래

english: This place is known for its hot springs. Want to check it out?
alt: I heard the hot springs here are amazing. Should we try them?
note: hot springs = 온천 / check it out = 가보다

korean: 여기서 일출 볼 수 있어? 몇 시에 해 떠?
english: Can we see the sunrise from here? What time does the sun come up?
alt: What time is sunrise? I want to catch it from this spot.

english: Where's the nearest station from here?
alt: Which train station is closest to this location?

english: Does this price include breakfast?
alt: Is breakfast included in the room rate?
note: include breakfast = 조식 포함 / room rate = 객실 요금

korean: 이거 예약금 환불 가능한가요? 일정이 바뀌어서요
english: Is the deposit refundable? My plans changed.
alt: Can I get my deposit back? Something came up and I can't make it.

korean: 이 호텔 조식 뷔페 맛있어? 포함된 거면 먹어야지
english: Is the hotel breakfast buffet any good? If it's included I might as well go.
alt: The complimentary breakfast better be worth waking up early for.
note: complimentary = 무료 / might as well = ~하는 게 낫다

korean: 온라인으로 체크인 했는데 탑승권 출력해야 하나요?
english: I checked in online. Do I still need to print my boarding pass?
alt: I already did online check-in. Should I get a printed pass?

korean: 짐 부칠 게 두 개인데 추가 요금 있어요?

alt: Is there any milk or cheese in this?

alt: Is there anything on the menu that's fully plant-based?

alt: Are there any tree nuts in this dish?

alt: What food is this place known for?

alt: The street food here is supposed to be amazing.

english: This is a bit salty for me.

alt: When does this place open and close?

alt: I'd like to verify my booking.

english: I'd like to book a double room.
alt: Can I reserve a room for two?

alt: What activities are there nearby?

alt: Do you have a city tour bus?

alt: What's the best trail to hike here?

alt: Can we take a gondola up?

alt: When does the sun rise here?

korean: 현지 공연 볼 수 있는 곳이 있나요?

alt: Where can I watch a traditional performance?

english: How much is the water park admission?
alt: What's the entry fee for the water park?

english: Is there a zoo nearby?
alt: Where's the closest zoo?

english: Where is the aquarium?
alt: How do I get to the aquarium?

alt: Is there a beginner scuba experience?

english: I want to try the zip line.
alt: Is there a zip line experience here?

english: Can I rent a kayak?
alt: Where can I go kayaking?

korean: 낚시 할 수 있는 곳이 있나요?

alt: Where can I fish around here?

alt: Where can I find a natural hot spring?

alt: Where's a good spot to get a massage?

alt: Have you been vaccinated for this trip?

alt: Be careful of altitude. Move slowly and stay hydrated.

alt: Don't forget your sunblock.

alt: How much are we spending on this vacation?

alt: We went over budget on this vacation.

alt: We should try to travel light this time.

alt: I need a voltage converter for my electronics.

alt: It's a Michelin-rated place.

alt: Where can I get photos developed?

alt: Is it okay to be out at night in this area?

alt: Convert to dollars first. Double exchanging loses value.

alt: Prices drop significantly during low season.

alt: Are there any water sports available here?

alt: I need to file a missing baggage claim.
note: lost luggage = missing baggage = 분실 수하물

alt: What's the refund policy for tour cancellations?

alt: Do they drive on the left in this country?

alt: How do I merge onto the freeway?

alt: Do I pump the gas myself?

alt: Which hospital has an ER close by?

alt: I need to cancel my card. It's been lost.

alt: Someone's injured. Where's the first aid kit?

alt: Fire! Everyone get out!

korean: 이 약 처방전 없이 살 수 있나요?

alt: Is this available over the counter?

english: Can I get some painkillers?
alt: I need something for pain.

english: Do you have anything for indigestion?
alt: Can I get some digestive medicine?

english: Do you have a Band-Aid?
alt: Can I get a bandage?

english: I got a bug bite and it's itchy.
alt: Something bit me and it's really itching.
note: bug bite = 벌레 물림 / itchy = 가려운

english: I got sunburned.
alt: My skin is burnt from the sun.

english: I've always wanted to come here. I finally made it.
alt: I've been dreaming of this place. We're finally here.

english: I need to organize my travel photos.
alt: I should sort through all the pictures from the trip.

korean: 이번 여행 최고였어 다음에 또 오자

alt: What an amazing trip. We have to do this again.

alt: I appreciate you dropping me off at the airport.

english: I'll let you know when I arrive safely.
alt: I'll text you once I land.
note: let you know = 알려주다 / land = 착륙하다

alt: Made it back safe and sound. What a trip.

alt: The jet lag is killing me.

alt: Time to write up the trip on my blog.

alt: Is a COVID test required for entry?
note: PCR test = PCR 검사 / entry requirements = 입국 요건

alt: Can I check one more piece of luggage?

english: This is fragile. Please handle it with care.
alt: Be careful with this. It's breakable.

english: Can I gate check the stroller?
alt: Do I check the stroller at the gate?

english: The in-flight entertainment system isn't working.
alt: My screen is frozen. Can someone reset it?

english: The passenger next to me keeps hogging the armrest.
alt: My neighbor won't share the armrest.

alt: The breakfast here got great reviews. Should we try it?

alt: Could I get an extra comforter? It's cold.

english: Can I order this through room service?

alt: Make sure to wash up before the meal. It's expected here.

english: In Western restaurants you shouldn't slurp or make noise while eating.
alt: Try not to eat loudly. It's considered rude here.

english: What time does this shop close?
alt: When do you close?

english: Can I bargain here?
